frameworks.name: multivoxel pattern analysis (MVPA) / representational similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: decoding feature-specific neural codes from distributed fMRI activity patterns

frameworks.name: univariate fMRI activation analysis
frameworks.one_line: voxel-wise mean-activation contrast that missed feature-specific encoding

core_question: Does human auditory cortex encode the direction and rate of rapid frequency modulation, and at what level (mean activation vs. distributed pattern)?
key_assumptions: iterated rippled noise carries phoneme-like FM features without being perceived as speech
distributed activity patterns can carry information invisible to mean-activation contrasts
passive listening engages auditory feature coding
manipulations: direction of frequency modulation (rising vs. falling sweeps)
rate of frequency modulation (fast vs. slow)
key_findings: univariate analysis: stronger and more extensive bilateral auditory-cortex activation for FM sweeps than steady sounds, left-lateralized
no univariate region showed selectivity for FM rate or direction
MVPA decoded direction of modulation bilaterally in auditory cortex
direction-decoding was strongest in regions outside Heschl's gyrus; no evidence for rate decoding
interpretations: FM direction is represented in fine-grained distributed patterns rather than overall activation level
non-primary auditory cortex carries the feature-specific code for FM direction
